title: Minuartia bannatica
taxonomy: Minuartia bannatica is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the pink or carnation family. The genus Minuartia is a group of small to medium-sized annual and perennial plants, and M. bannatica is a specific species within this genus. M. bannatica is native to Bulgaria and is found growing in areas with rocky outcrops and dry soils. This species is typically characterized by its small, white flowers and narrow leaves. It is often classified as a member of the subgenus Arenaria within the genus Minuartia.

intro: Minuartia bannatica, also known as Bann's pearlwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and gastrointestinal problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia bannatica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in phytomedicine to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections.
